name: Secured Mastercard
effective_cost: Free, with no annual fee; refundable deposit sets your credit line

verdict.text: Straightforward credit-building card with a clear graduation path

earn_note: The Citi Secured Mastercard earns no cash back or points. Citi's own product page for this card mentions only optional Merchant Offers (opt-in, targeted discounts at select retailers), not a real rewards currency. If you want a secured card that earns actual cash back, that's not this issuer's secured product; Capital One's Quicksilver Secured is the closer match.

points.note: No points or cash-back program of any kind. The entire value proposition is the refundable security deposit, monthly reporting to all three credit bureaus, and a defined path to an unsecured Citi card.

transfer_partners.highlight: No rewards currency exists on this card, so there is nothing to transfer.

protection_note: Bare-bones by design: Citi's page for this card doesn't advertise purchase protection, extended warranty, or travel insurance. What you get instead is $0 liability on unauthorized charges and Mastercard ID Theft Protection, the same baseline fraud tools Citi puts on all of its cards.
rental_note: No rental car coverage advertised; use a different card for rentals.

services.name: Refundable security deposit sets your credit line
services.detail: A $200–$2,500 deposit, in $100 increments, opens the account at that exact credit line; you choose how much to put down within that range.

services.name: Path to an unsecured card
services.detail: Citi reviews accounts for possible graduation to the unsecured Diamond Preferred card as early as 9 months (early review) or by 12 months (standard review) of responsible use; the deposit is refunded on graduation.

services.name: Credit bureau reporting
services.detail: Reports payment activity to all three major credit bureaus every month, the core reason to hold a secured card.

services.name: APR
services.detail: Variable rate based on creditworthiness, reported in the mid-to-high-20s% by recent reviews. Citi doesn't publish an exact figure on its public summary page, so check your card agreement for the precise rate.

services.name: Foreign transaction fee applies
services.detail: 3% of the U.S. dollar amount of each transaction.

services.name: Free FICO Score access
services.detail: View your FICO Score at no cost through the Citi app or site.

services.name: Citi Quick Lock
services.detail: Instantly lock and unlock the card from the app if it's misplaced.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Can help build the authorized user's credit history too
No rewards earned: this card has no rewards program to share

additional_cards.note: Free to add, and since this account reports to all three bureaus, an authorized user can benefit from the payment history even though there's no rewards program to share.
